reasoning: Peer set (Denzi helmets) uses varied warm-cool balance with grays, reds, golds, and teal accents; candidate is monolithically cool blue-cyan with almost no warm tones — color temperature mismatch is notable. Outline weight appears roughly consistent (1px dark), and rendering style is both pixel art, so those dims hold. Shadow direction is ambiguous on candidate's narrow tower form, only 1 visible shadow zone vs peers' 2-3. Highlight intensity: peers show crisp bright white/light highlight spots; candidate's blue palette mutes highlight contrast significantly. Palette overlap: only the teal/cyan helmet peers share any hue overlap, leaving ~70% of the peer palette unrepresented. Detail density: candidate has a simple striped/segmented column, peers pack more internal detail per pixel-area. Proportion: candidate is extremely tall-narrow (approx 4:1 H:W); peers are compact ~1:1 — item-type difference noted but the silhouette reads as 'generic asset' not 'Denzi charm'. Charm level: peer helmets project personality (horns, crests, faces implied); checkpoint tower is utilitarian with no personality features, falling noticeably short of peer CHARM LEVEL. Overall: style-engine is correct (pixel art, dark outline) but palette, temperature, charm, and highlight execution are divergent enough to flag as marginal.
